import source


def test_source_module_imports():
    assert source is not None
def test_filter_expensive_items_with_string_prices():
    """Test that filter_expensive_items handles string prices correctly."""
    items = [
        {'name': 'item1', 'price': 100},
        {'name': 'item2', 'price': '200'},
        {'name': 'item3', 'price': 50},
        {'name': 'item4', 'price': '300'},
    ]
    threshold = 150
    
    # The fixed code should convert string prices to int before comparison
    # or handle the type coercion properly. It should return items with
    # price > 150, which are items with prices 200 and 300.
    result = source.filter_expensive_items(items, threshold)
    
    # Expected result after fixing: items with prices > 150
    # item2 has price '200' which is > 150 when converted to int
    # item4 has price '300' which is > 150 when converted to int
    # item1 has price 100 (<= 150) and item3 has price 50 (<= 150)
    expected_items = [
        {'name': 'item2', 'price': '200'},
        {'name': 'item4', 'price': '300'},
    ]
    
    assert result == expected_items
